sentences.text: How's it going, Brad?
Good, Nathan, how are you?
I'm doing well, doing well.
It's Friday.
You got any big plans for the weekend?
Some R&R.
We just had an acquisition this week, expanding our portfolio into Sanford, Florida, and it has been… 10-12 hour days all week, so just kicking my feet up and relaxing a little bit.
How about you?
Yeah, I, we had a work event Monday, Tuesday, and Wednesday this week, up in Chicago.
So… my plans for the weekend, same as yours.
Relax.
Nice.
Put my feet up, recover….
Yeah.
I've got a, I've got a 4-month-old at home.
So, like, he's at least sleeping through the night.
He sleeps from 8pm to 7am, which is amazing, but he's been sick for a little over a month, and, like, wakes himself up, like, coughing so much in the middle of the night.
But I'm sorry to hear that.
It's all good.
We, we took him to the doctor yesterday, and we're like, you're gonna have to do something to help us get this fixed, so….
We all need to sleep.
Do what?
I said, we all need to sleep, you and the baby.
Yeah, I know, I know.
It's like, every, every night, it's like clockwork, it's like 3 AM, coughing and, like, choking himself away.
all.
Then he just… he goes straight back to sleep, but it's… you can… The cough is just to the point, like, where you can tell his throat's dry, and….
Yeah.
site hurts a little bit, so hopefully this medicine fixes it, and he's good to go, so….
Yeah.
For sure.
So… Awesome.
Well, thank you guys for hopping on.
I know, Brad, you mentioned you guys had a lot going on, which I'm assuming it was the Florida stuff, so….
Yeah, one of many things, but yeah, that's the one that consumed us most this week.
Yeah.
Well, it's the weekend, so I won't keep you long.

I know, I know you got a lot going on, so… I guess the way I envisioned the call going, I had a couple of questions for you guys about your portfolio, what you were seeing, what you were doing, and then I know you were interested in hearing what we had to offer, so I'll dive in and explain that to you as well.
Yeah, that'd be great.
Cool.
So you just… how many of… how many properties did you just acquire?
Yeah, we just acquired one, but it was 352 units in Sanford.
Brings our portfolio up to, like, 34, because we've had a couple dispositions this year.
35 inch, we don't know anymore.
It's a small number, but we can never keep track.
Right?
We sell them, we buy them, we build them, we never.
So you guys own and manage everything then, right?
Correct.
Yes.
Yep.
Okay.
Okay, cool.
That at least makes your guys' lives a little bit easier, not having to deal with 15 different ownership groups.
Yeah, I, one of the biggest… property management companies in this market that's probably on rentable now, town properties.

I was a regional vice president with them for a long time, and I had 11 different owners and 26 properties I was responsible for, and it was… you know, inevitably, the property, or the owner with, you know, 88 units out in BFE on the other side of, like, Gainesville, Ohio, is the one consuming 15% of my week.
And they're 1% of my portfolio.
That's… yeah.
That's so time-consuming.
Yeah.
I have to do something like that.
So, yeah, I, … That's… yeah, you're at least fortunate there.
I mean, you gotta go down to Florida and whatnot, but… at least you got a pod there, it seems like, so….
Yeah.
So, cool.
When it comes to advertising, what do you do right now?
I know you're on Apartments.
com, but….
Yeah, it is a very strategic approach by … Product type, sub-market, … you know, the aggressiveness of the property, if we're doing renovation.
I mean, every single Property has a curated marketing approach.
Okay.
We don't just blanket sign, you know, everyone up on one platform.
Which is… Exception of, basically, Zumper.
Everything's just kind of hand-picked.
Yeah, and even that one doesn't have everything, but we're trying to.
Yeah, it's a very prescriptive… Type approach, which is great for the properties, allows us to be intentional, but it's a ton of work for us, especially when we're changing.
I mean, we just got out of a one and a half hour meeting with CoStar just a few minutes ago.

They came and met us here at the office, and… Went through our portfolio with them, and just… you know, different opportunities, and we've been setting up some properties on Zillow, we've been adding more to ApartmentList and trying out ApartmentList Lyft on a few of our properties right now, being strategic with our SEM and pay-per-click advertising,
trying different vendors for that.
Yeah, you name it.
No, that makes sense, yeah.
I mean, not every market is the same, not every sub-market's the same, so, I mean, I totally understand the approach.
… What are you seeing?
How's renter traffic been?
How's renter demand been lately?
an occupancy.
It's… Similarly to the conversation of our approach, it's very varied in sub-markets.
Right now, we we are throwing so much money at Naples, Florida right now, just to get a lease, and we are on… Diamond Plus on Apartments.
com with them.
We are spending… Oh.
high pay-per-lease amounts with department lists, we are sending direct mailer postcards, we're running social campaigns, SEM campaigns, … we're doing everything we can in Naples.
Florida itself has been a bear, a little bit shocking that we decided to acquire in Sanford, but Orlando's been a struggle.
And then some of our lease subs, I mean, just the Cincinnati area, the greater Cincinnati area, varies wildly just by some of the sub-markets.
We had one product that we felt really, really good about, two years ago in Springboro, Ohio, just, you know, the suburb of Dayton, that struggled.
Really, really struggled.
I mean, it took us a year and a half.
It was the never-ending lease-up.
And it was only 168 units.
We turned around and redid that exact same product in Hamilton, Ohio, to the tune of 272 units, and crushed it.
It's one of our best lease-ups ever.
And they're the exact same style.
35 minutes apart.
That's absurd.
Yo.
Yeah, Delhi, out in… The west side of Cincinnati, at a lower-income market, Viridian.
Rented itself.

Yeah, the pent-up demand, they hadn't had any new product type, single-family… multifamily, excuse me, Class A-ish product in over 40 years, and the pent-up demand, as soon as we turned the switch on, the property filled itself.
I hate….

Factory 52, our crown jewel right now, is struggling, and it's in the heart of everything, But it… it's… it's getting a decent amount of leads, and there might be some additional challenges with You know, just training and on-site teams and stuff like that, but… It's been moving slower than we thought it would.
Because the lease up itself, 2 years ago.
Yeah.
similarly, just filled up.

We raised rents a half a dozen times, we just added 128 units to that project with a new building, and it's taking… it's not doing bad, it's still beating… It's just inching along, which is slow for factory.
That's not what happened with Phase 1.
We're used to 6, 8, 10 leases a week, and right now we're getting, like, 1 to 2 for that new building, which, again, they're still ahead of.
Their goals, but….
If we, yeah, if we have another month of this, we'll actually start getting behind, so….
Yeah, yeah.
Okay, so it sounds like there's some areas that could use some help, ….
But overall, I mean, like, you guys are doing….
a lot, right?
Yeah.
You're doing… you're doing everything you pretty much can.
There's a… there's a couple areas where we can fill in the gaps.
… One of them is…
Like, one of the main focuses that we have is filling in gaps and trying to get your properties everywhere that they're not.
So, like, in your instance, right, like, you have a very strategic plan for every sub-market, and we can kind of… Get your property on the sites that it's not utilizing.
I'll dive in and make it… make a lot more sense.
But, … But yeah, I think there's… let's see here…
Some areas where we could potentially help.
Can you see this okay?
Yep.
Okay, so… with everything that we offer, we're still an ILS first and foremost.
Right?
So, just like in Apartments.

com or a Zillow, couple of standout differences, one of them being the fact that we're super cost-effective, and We're not gonna be a huge dent in the marketing… in the marketing budget compared to… Yeah.
Platinum Plus.
… So… When we bring on your properties, it looks like you're using Resman, is that right?
Correct.
Okay.
So we integrate with ResMond, so anytime you're updating information on your end, it'll pull and reflect on ours.
And then, from there, we'll advertise your property on Rentable in itself, which you're not getting on.
And then from our site, we're gonna syndicate your property out.
We have one of the industry's largest networks, and the reason I say that is because we're basically syndicating to everywhere except for Apartments.
com.
Now, when it comes to it, in your case, right, I'll talk portfolio-wide, because it'll make the most sense that way.
We have an AI tool working in the background to make sure we're not duplicating where you're posting.

So, like, if you have a property on Zumper, for an example, for, like, 15 of your 35-ish properties, we wouldn't duplicate and push there for those 15, but for all the properties that weren't utilizing it, we would push and fill in the gaps there.
Same thing goes for, like, apartment lists in Zillow.
We won't push there if you're already there, but for the properties that aren't, we can fill in the gaps and get you there, though, that way.
Does that make sense?
Kinda?
So, if we're not advertising on an ILS, with the exception of Apartments.
com, Rentable puts us on that platform somehow.
Correct.
Interesting.
How does readable… yeah, how?
Yeah, so that's the… that's the big question.

We have a channel, a back-end, like, channel partnership agreement with them, so essentially what we're doing is we syndicate and we're able to list on the sites, and then we're paying for those leads from those sites for you.
Do you have an example of what that would look like?
Like, a rentable….
It would look the same exact way as a normal listing.
So it looks like we were on apartment lists, even if we weren't.
Exactly.
Interesting, okay.
So the lead comes in from apartment list or rentable?
So, the lead would come in from apartment list to us, and then we would send it over to you as a guest card, and it would all come in as a rentable lead.
We typically bucket as.
Not on the platform.
Right.
We bucket it as one of three things.
We bucket it as rentable, rentable affiliate, or paid social on the back end.
So, and that's what I meant earlier when I said we're essentially filling in the gaps and getting you everywhere that you're not.
We're not here to tell you to leave a current agreement by any means, but if you made the decision to do so, then we can… we can kind of pick up where they're leaving off.
Interesting.
So if we left… so if a property's not on Stillow.
it would show up on Zillow.
It'd come to them as a Zillow, even to us as a one.
And then… I guess I just need to Google, like, what the… like, what it looks like, you know what I mean?
Yeah, I had it up.
Did you?
Yeah, it… you… like, if you were searching and you found it on Zillow, for an example, it wouldn't appear any different than the way it would if you had a direct agreement.
The… the main difference with the way we syndicate is we let the paid subscriptions take precedent, because they're gonna make sure that you're showing up on page 1 Whatever it may be.
With ours, it's gonna push over as, like, more of a middle-upper tier, so it's gonna be, like, a page 2, 3, 4.
Okay, cool.
So, and that's the biggest difference.
You're not gonna show up on page 15, 16, 17 with our syndication by any means, but we're not gonna promise you the diamond level.
Yeah.
Fuck.
The third key component that we provide is an all-encompassing targeted social ad.
For each one of your properties.

So… It'll kind of double down on any social campaigns you're running right now, but essentially, anytime somebody goes on a search engine, says, show me apartments in Cincinnati, or a suburb of Cincinnati, or… Naples, Florida.
Next time they get on their Facebook or Instagram, we'll hit them with a targeted rentable ad that looks like this.
It'll have pictures of your individual properties.
We'll have some details about it.
It's pet-friendly, in-unit laundry.
Oh.
Lowest advertised price, whatever it may be.
Yeah.
The way our social ads differ from what other companies do.
Dude.
Other companies will either redirect them, a renter to their site to see all the other listings that they're They're advertising for, all the other companies.
Or, they'll redirect them to a property website, which at that point, you kind of lose traction of, did that person become a lead?
Did it come from here?
What's going on?

All of ours are lead generating, so once they click learn more, we're gonna have them fill out a form with their name, their email, their phone number, and we're gonna deliver that to you as a guest card.
And then when we deliver your guest cards, it's either going to go into your… are you using ResMin for your CRM, too?
We don't really have a CRM right now.
We are vetting out a couple, we're just using the antiquated Resman funnel.
Okay.
So, we can send it to a CRM, or we can send it to an on-site leasing email address, or both, it's whichever you.
Can you send to two email addresses?
Yep.
So, whoa, sorry.
My MacBook loves to animate things that I do every once in a while, so….
Yeah, I get the… I get the thumbs up one… all the time.
Yeah, I just found out you can do a heart, apparently?
It, like… Amazing.
Anyways, … Yeah, the heart doesn't… maybe I'm not lovable.
I got sidetracked for a second, what were you just talking about?
We were talking about social ads, sending leads to CRMs, or to….
Oh, yeah.

So, if we can send it to two email addresses, we have a parsing email that would put the lead directly in… I need to stop using my hands… put the lead directly into ResMin, and then the secondary or the CC email address would send the lead directly to the on-site team.
Correct.
Okay.
Yes.
Exactly.
So… Super simplistic, super easy, all the posts are AI-generated as well, so you don't have to go through and create anything on your end.
… Yep.
Awesome.
Got any other questions for me?
I don't think so.
This is, so far been a great kind of discovery call, because we admittedly don't know a ton about rentable, but I do… after being with Town Properties, I do remember having a lot of our portfolio on.
Yeah, yeah.
I'm trying to think… when were you with Town Properties?
Like, back in 2023?
No, it's been longer than that.

I don't know if we were on… truth be told, I don't know if we were on it back then, but I… looking at it now, most of the properties, when I get on the rentable platform are properties that used to be in my portfolio.
Oh, okay.
Yeah.
So I don't know if we were on it back when I was there.
That was… would have been, like, 2000… 16 to 2022, 2021?
Yeah, let's see, … I can tell ya.
Back in… 2022.
So, yeah, it was probably right after I left.
2021 as well.
Probably signed the contract.
Yes.
20, 2019… Yeah, it looked like town properties, so it just kept expanding.
It started in 2018, and then went all the way…
Yeah, kept batting all the way through 2022.
Yeah.
Well, that's promising.
They kept adding more properties, and they didn't….
Yeah, and that was before we started doing the all-inclusive targeted social ad piece as well.
And before we grew our syndicate network.
back then, I think our major points were Facebook Marketplace andare.
com.
I remember that.
Yep.
So…
Had a lot of growth since then, for the better.
We've worked with… I'm trying to… I pulled up some pro… … some people in your area, like PK Management, we work with Woda Cooper up in Ohio.
Winterwood Residential out of Lexington.
Which, they have some properties, I believe, that go through Cincinnati.
Okay.
… We have… we performed pretty well through there.
Tm Associates?
They have quite a few properties through Ohio.
They're based out of DC.
… I lost the other name, but there's one other big one too, but….
It's all good.
I guess the only thing I have left for you guys is pricing.
That's always a big one.
It is.
So, in terms of when coming on board with us, we do require a 5-property minimum.
Okay.
However, the price is only $199 a month per property.
So, when I said pennies to the dollar compared to an Apartments.
com spend, I wasn't joking.
Yeah.
What are some of your hottest markets?
What are some of our hottest markets?
Yeah, like, where is rentable really, like, the shining star as an ILS in the country?
So, we've had a lot of success, and I'm not just saying this because this is your area, we've had a lot of success through Ohio, DC, Florida.
Texas has been a big market for us.
Yeah.
… Wisconsin's been a big market for us.
So, ….
Mountain West, Southeast, or, yeah, those are….
Okay.
Tell me more about your, paint social, just for shits and giggles, … How many posts per week All the stuff.
Is it all paid ad spend, or do they show up as organic, like the property's just posting?
No, it's, it's… it's all, pops up as ads, so we don't do anything with Facebook Marketplace anymore.
Back in 20… I think it was, like, February 23, they changed their policies where companies couldn't have a direct feed into Facebook Marketplace anymore.
Yep.
So… It's 3 postings a week, … the postings are AI-generated.
We put a… a radius on them as well, … like, 25 mile to 40 mile radius, so that way somebody looking in Lexington for a property is.
I'm gonna.
your property's in Cincinnati?
Yeah.
So….
If it's 3 posts a week, are those 3 different ad campaigns that are running?
Or… because an ad campaign, you turn it on, you just leave it run.
Yeah, yeah, yeah, so it's, it's just, it's running constantly.
We never turn it off, but it should be 3 separate ads.
Constantly running.
And we refresh them, and you have to, like, re-update them, too.
Okay.

So, and the reason… the reason we do that is because the same AI tool that we have running, that's creating the content, it's making sure that we're not duplicating listings, it's… making sure and figuring out which pictures that we post are getting the better click rates.
So, like, if it's a picture of your pool.
Yeah.
Or clicks in the picture of the kitchen, for an example, we're gonna move that pool to the front.
Of the picture list more often than we have the kitchen.
Okay.
Just to figure out, like, what's getting the most clicks, what's getting the most engagement, so we can constantly get results for you.
Same thing with our syndicate network, too, right?
Like, if we're syndicating to 30 sites, we want to make sure it's the sites that are getting you the top results.
Yeah.

Depending on the suburb or whatnot, it'll say, here's your, like, top 3 sites that we're getting you on, here's your bottom 3 sites, so that way, instead of us trying to hit the… just constantly beat a dead horse over here on these bottom three.
We can take what spin we would be using there and just put it towards the top 3 sites to double down on the efforts and results.
Okay.
If a property's running a special, would you put that in one of your ads?
So… Yes and no.
We wouldn't just, like, come out and, like, put it in there, but if you have, like, a photo.
representing, talking about the special, we can make sure that it's getting pulled into it.
You just have to send it over to us.
Okay, because typically you'd pull the photos from the resident feed, but if we sent you one-offs, you would put it in the mix?
Correct.
Cool.

And that photo… would have to have all of the special in the infographics, so if it's a picture of the pool and you put text on top of it that says, one month free, must move in, blah blah blah, would there be, then, custom copy written to accompany that?
So… no.
So, like, the same bullets would apply.
We can't really, like, edit those and say special, ….
Okay.
But, in terms of, like, pulling the picture, we can make sure that's pulled, and then we can set that to being one of the first photos in the mix.
the next.
Like, rent special $1,000 off for the month.
Whatever it may be.
Yeah.
Okay.
Great questions.
How… how does all of this compare to what you're doing right now?
It's different, right?
I think you guys know that you're the ones that could fill, kind of, those gaps where we're not signing ILS partners up for every single platform, or properties up on every single ILS platform.
Right.
We don't have any… But we do have some paid social ad spend throughout their iOSes.
You mean my face with us?
That's… but we're doing that in-house.
In-house, yeah.
Yeah, not through an ILS partner.
… Minimum commitment, I know there's 5 properties, is it… can we do a 3-month trial for 5 properties, or…?
We normally do annual agreements, but right now we're running a special for as low as 6 months, so I can do as short as a 6-month term.
Thank you.
Okay.
What's that?
It's a $6,000 minimal commitment.
Yeah.
Which is 1 month spend on an Apartments.
com for 3 properties.
Yeah.
At least, depending on the platform.
Right.
for the tier.
Yeah.
Is all of this something you can send over to us after the call?
Yeah, yeah, yeah, absolutely.
I can send pricing, bullet points, everything we've discussed.
Yeah, I think, after the week we've had, this is all good, just to kind of… Put in the old brain, but it's something we'll want to visit, you know, next week or the following week, one week.
When you're a little… when you've got a little more energy, and….
Exactly.
Yeah.
We're brain dead, but interested.
Yeah, that's a good way to put it.
I get it.
We're just sitting here like, please hurry up and be 5 o'clock already.
The only other things I can touch on is when you come on board with us, you do have a dedicated success manager.
They'll do monthly check-in calls, or every other week check-in calls, however frequent you want it, just to help ensure that we're meeting the goals that you have set.
Okay.
The other thing is you would have access to a backend dashboard where you can keep track of lead volume, what's being sent over, everything.
So, the reason I mention that is because you mentioned at the beginning, like, how some properties may have some… maybe it's, like, some on-site team issues, whatever it may be.
If you need to, like, monitor what they're doing a little more, you can kind of do that.
And here I'll show you what this looks like.
Like…
It's awesome.
It's a very simplistic dashboard.
It's not too complicated.
So when you log in, you would see a list of all the properties you have active.
You can go into Analytics here.
And you can see, over the past 30 days, however many leads were sent, so once it loads, it'll show you.
So, like, in the past 30 days, we've sent 649 leads.
Rose Tower, we've sent 143 to.
Orangeburg Manor, 155.
87 of these were emails, 56 were calls.
You can go back and change the past 90 days.
Or 120, whatever it is, year.
It'll analyze 1,852 in the past 90 days.
You can also go in and see what the leads were.
So, you can say, okay, cool, today at 922, John Roberts applied to Rose Tower, here's his email, here's exactly what he said.
And then you can go into calls as well on 7-16, Rose Tower is called by this number, you can go in and listen to how that conversation sounded.
Do you guys post a Google business profile?
I see that on the left there.
So, we do have a product, that does it, so it's not rentable, it's our Maven AI product.
Save more.
We're running test pilots right now with Maven AI on two of our properties.
Yeah, I was about to say, Maven AI is our sister product.
I, I think you're talking, I think, what is it, Kelsey?
Kelsey and Patrick.
Kelsey and Patrick.
Yeah, Kelsey and Patrick's awesome.
… We used to sell all the products together as one, and then Maven got so big that we're like, okay, we gotta separate things, so we give a different product specialist for it.
Any bundled package savings, if we went, like, with both Maven and Rentable?
I'd have to talk with them, Patrick's able to… to let me know on that.
To my knowledge, we've done it before in the past.
Okay.
It may go down… I… what did they quote you?
99 a property as well?
Yeah, I think that's what it was, because it's… it matches Zumper's pricing, and Zumper gets the GBP post and the ILS platform access for $200 a month.
We're just test piloting in a free 45-day pilot with Mark Lane and Lionsgate with Maven AI.
Gotcha, gotcha.
We may be able to do something where it drops down to, like, $175.
Each, er, yeah.
Versus 199.
I'd have to talk with Patrick to get.
Definitely.
Yeah.
They don't give me that, … That power.
No, I get that, but, you know, just thinking as budget season approaches, if it's something that we can start at least curing, We've been very happy with Zumper so far.
… But, you know, if there's opportunity to get more for a nominal Amount more, we'll humor it.
For sure.
How's the Maven AI pilot going so far?
Good, I mean, it's only been live for a little over a week, maybe 2 weeks now, at most.
But posts are great, they're pulling through specials, doing them as GDP offers, which… everything looks good.
Perfect.
Yeah.
That's awesome to hear.
It's awesome to hear.
So… I guess in terms of advertising.
What, what does next steps look like for you?

… I don't mean to sound so defeated, it's just… Yeah, going into budgeting season, I don't… if there's a possibility to run a 6-month trial with 5 properties sooner rather than later, that's what I would like to do.
But, you know, each regional would have to be involved for the properties that we select.
Okay.
To be 100%, the money's already there for some of the properties, because instead of running Facebook ads, we could do rentable.
And then that takes things off mine and Abby's plate, also, which I love.
And then they'll still put the Facebook advertising up for us.
Yep.
Yeah.
So, you actually… It won't be as targeted.
There'll be more.
AI driven.
Like, right now, we can run them for specific floor plans and specific expressions.
Sometimes that's actually better.
Depending.
Yeah.
But sometimes it is.
And we still have the ability to run them on top of that, if we need to do that.
Exactly.
You could double down.
For lease-ups and stuff.
I mean… Yeah, so if we can… I like it.
Ideally, I mean, I would love to do a 3-month pilot.
I know that's often not enough time for… To get a full, you know, absorption of the product and the platform, but… We think we could probably find 5 properties for at least 6 months, maybe more.
And I said I'd like to do shorter, just because if it's something we truly love, I'd like to budget for it.
For more properties in 2026.
So, we'll do our best to make sure the money's there.
Yeah, can you get back to us if you would let us do 5 properties just at the end of the year?
Is that August or September?
my smoke trial.
5-month….
It'd be less than that.
I can go and I can ask.
Yeah.
Don't ask to get!
Yeah, I, I will… they…
I don't want to give you false hope, because I have not been able to get anything less than 6 months approved in the past year, so….
Even if we can move forward with 6, we can lose budget for the first couple months.
Yeah, and then we can play by ear.
I, I'd be….

And then, as you scale with us, too, so, like, let's… let's talk positive, like, you do this, and… goes really well, you want to add more properties, as you scale, the more properties you get up to, like, we offer discounts off.
So….
Okay.
What's the next tier, or does it depend on our whole portfolio size, or…?
It depends on portfolio size.
So, like, if you got up to 20 properties, it would go down to $175 a month per property.
Normally, it's 40 is the next one, but you have 35, so we could probably work something out where 35 gets you down to, like, $150 a month per property.
Okay.
Who knows, we might be there sooner rather than later.
But we sell them as fast as we buy them and build them, so it's like….
Yeah.
We can also put, because you do buy and sell a lot, I can put a clause in there too, like 30 day out if the property were to sell or to change of management.
Excellent.
That way you're not stuck with an agreement for a property you don't own.
I'm gonna be honest, I didn't want to get on this call just because it's lunchtime, and my head is, like.
But I'm really glad we did, because this is kinda cool.
Definitely.
I'm glad you guys decided to get on.
Yeah, I know, lunch.
